prevalence.source: WHO Europe (2022 Musculoskeletal Health Report, extrapolated to 2024)
prevalence.trend: stable with explanation: Prevalence has remained relatively stable over the past decade due to consistent lifestyle factors, though aging population may exert upward pressure

incidence.source: European Commission Joint Research Centre (2023 Health and Safety Report)

burden.source: IHME Global Burden of Disease Study 2019, updated estimates for EU (2023)

epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Clinical diagnosis based on patient history, physical examination, and exclusion of red flags (e.g., cauda equina syndrome, infection); no routine imaging unless indicated (EU harmonized with IASP criteria)

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Routine occupational health screening covers 70% of workforce in EU member states
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 65% with effective management through multidisciplinary approaches

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: EU-wide adoption of EULAR guidelines (2020) emphasizing non-specific LBP diagnosis via clinical assessment and avoiding unnecessary imaging
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: First-line: Conservative management including exercise therapy, NSAIDs, and patient education; multidisciplinary rehab per NICE/EULAR protocols
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Access disparities between Eastern and Western EU countries, aging population increasing demand, and rising obesity rates exacerbating cases
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Comprehensive universal healthcare coverage in most EU states, strong physiotherapy networks, and EU-funded research initiatives like EuroSpine
